jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer's daily routine
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation after presumably crashing or making a mistake
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase, perfectly integrated into his character and instantly recognizable. High quotability and impact.

jokes.text: Go-Go Ray demonstration making someone dance uncontrollably
jokes.setup: Science fair project demonstration
jokes.punchline: Ray makes someone do the Monkey dance against their will
jokes.explanation: Solid physical comedy setup with absurd science fair invention. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I'm disrupting the learning process, and I love it!
jokes.setup: Person forced to dance by the Go-Go Ray
jokes.punchline: Gleeful admission of disruption while helpless to stop
jokes.explanation: Good ironic twist - embracing the chaos they can't control. Decent character moment.

jokes.text: Why are you saying that? / Just screwin' with your mind.
jokes.setup: Lisa repeatedly saying 'First prize!'
jokes.punchline: Bart casually admits he's just messing with her head
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart behavior - casual cruelty delivered matter-of-factly. Good character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Yeah. Syrup is better than jelly.
jokes.setup: Marge asking Homer to encourage Lisa with her science project
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely irrelevant and random food comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer non-sequitur. Shows his inability to focus or understand the situation. Good character work.

jokes.text: The kind that help our Olympic athletes reach new peaks of excellence? / The very same.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentioning anabolic steroids for her tomato project
jokes.punchline: Homer's naive positive spin on steroid use in athletics
jokes.explanation: Good satirical jab at steroid scandals in sports. Homer's innocent endorsement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: More tomato? / Yes, please. / Yes, please.
jokes.setup: Lisa's giant steroid-enhanced tomato solving world hunger
jokes.punchline: Multiple people politely accepting more tomato in foreign accent
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing the absurd success of Lisa's project. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Little do they know, I'm ducking out early to take the Duff Brewery tour.
jokes.setup: Homer pretending to go to work
jokes.punchline: His 'secret' plan to skip work for beer tour
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's main plot. Perfect Homer behavior - choosing beer over work responsibility.

jokes.text: Heh, heh, heh! They don't suspect a thing.
jokes.setup: Homer thinks he's cleverly hiding his plan to skip work for the brewery tour
jokes.punchline: He just announced his plan out loud to his family
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - his confidence despite obvious failure is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Uh-oh. Did I say that or just think it?
jokes.setup: Homer's internal monologue about his plan
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall by questioning his own thought process
jokes.explanation: Clever meta moment that works perfectly with Homer's character. Shows his mental confusion in a funny way.

jokes.text: Homer, are you going to the Duff Brewery? / [Shrieks] [Door slams]
jokes.setup: Homer caught off guard by Marge's question
jokes.punchline: Panic scream and immediate escape
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Homer's guilty panic. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: If the plant ye wish to flee, go to Sector 7-B.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to escape the nuclear plant
jokes.punchline: Rhyming escape instructions in olde English
jokes.explanation: Clever rhyming format gives mundane direction comedic weight. Good wordplay structure.

jokes.text: To overcome the spider's curse, simply quote a Bible verse. / Thou shalt not-- / D'oh!
jokes.setup: Rhyming instruction for escaping spider
jokes.punchline: Homer fails immediately and gets his signature exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good setup-payoff structure. Homer's failure and 'D'oh!' callback works well. Nice escalation of the escape sequence.

jokes.text: Hey. That looks like Princess Di. / Aw, wait. It's just a pile of rags.
jokes.setup: Homer seeing something that resembles Princess Diana
jokes.punchline: Deflating revelation that it's just rags
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection joke structure. Shows Homer's tendency toward wishful thinking and pop culture awareness.

jokes.text: Soitenly. Nyuk, nyuk, nyuk!
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Bart to hold something
jokes.punchline: Bart doing Three Stooges impression
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference. Fits Bart's character but lacks originality. Functional background humor.

jokes.text: Over, under, in and out. That's what shoe tying's all about.
jokes.setup: Bart tying his shoe
jokes.punchline: Making shoe-tying sound like a profound philosophy
jokes.explanation: Mildly clever wordplay making mundane activity sound important. Fits Bart's casual attitude.

jokes.text: Huh? / [Distorted] No!
jokes.setup: Bart doing something with his shoelaces
jokes.punchline: Distorted scream suggesting he got tangled or caught
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff to the shoe-tying setup. Simple slapstick that works for the character.

jokes.text: Now, I'm sure all of you have heard the rumors that a batch of Duff was contaminated with strychnine. / Strychnine? / Well, everyone's talking about it. It was even on CNN last night.
jokes.setup: Tour guide mentioning contamination rumors
jokes.punchline: Tour guide accidentally creates the rumor by bringing it up
jokes.explanation: Excellent example of how trying to squash a rumor spreads it. Good corporate satire and ironic structure.

jokes.text: Duff Beer! Proud sponsors of Amos 'N' Andy.
jokes.setup: 1950s Duff commercial
jokes.punchline: Sponsoring a now-controversial racist radio show
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical jab at corporate history and changing social standards. Economical and biting.

jokes.text: The man never drank a Duff in his life.
jokes.setup: Political figure endorsing Duff beer on TV
jokes.punchline: Tour guide's bitter aside about the insincerity
jokes.explanation: Good satire of celebrity endorsements and political hypocrisy. Economical and realistic character moment.

jokes.text: What does the future hold for Duff? / Like what? / I'd rather not get into it right now. / Why not? / All right. We don't have any ideas for the future. We got nothing. Happy? / No.
jokes.setup: Tour guide teasing future plans for Duff
jokes.punchline: Complete breakdown admitting they have no ideas at all
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure showing corporate emptiness. Homer's persistence forces uncomfortable honesty.

jokes.text: Help me! Help me! / What's so funny? / I was just thinkin' of a joke I saw on Herman's Head.
jokes.setup: Hamster apparently in distress
jokes.punchline: Homer referencing obscure sitcom instead of addressing the situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness. Pop culture reference that shows his misplaced priorities and poor timing.

jokes.text: Fine. Fine. Mouse. Fine. Mouse. / Rat. Fine. Syringe. Fine. Nose. Fine.
jokes.setup: Quality control at beer factory
jokes.punchline: Casually approving increasingly disturbing contaminants
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy escalation. Each item gets progressively worse but gets the same casual approval. Great corporate satire.

jokes.text: Let me just say you're doin' a great job, Phil. / Hey, thanks a lot. That makes it all worthwhile.
jokes.setup: Phil approving obviously contaminated beer
jokes.punchline: Phil genuinely appreciating the compliment despite his terrible work
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing how people crave validation regardless of performance. Nice ironic contrast.

jokes.text: Mmm! Gummi Beer.
jokes.setup: Homer sampling brewery products
jokes.punchline: Discovering candy version of beer with signature satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer food reaction. The 'Mmm!' is his signature response. Absurd product concept works well.

jokes.text: Are you crazy? We still haven't tried Raspberry Duff, Lady Duff, Tartar Control Duff--
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting Barney has had enough to drink
jokes.punchline: Barney listing increasingly ridiculous beer varieties
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of absurd product varieties. Tartar Control Duff is particularly funny. Perfect for Barney's character.

jokes.text: I'm trying to knock you out. / Ow! Cut it out! / Ow! Ow!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to knock out drunk Barney
jokes.punchline: Homer's ineffective attempts and Barney's casual complaints
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Homer's incompetence and Barney's casual attitude toward violence.

jokes.text: Look alive, boys. Couple of stewed prunes headin' your way.
jokes.setup: Police preparing for drunk drivers
jokes.punchline: Creative slang for drunk people as 'stewed prunes'
jokes.explanation: Creative police slang that's both descriptive and funny. Good wordplay with 'stewed' having double meaning.

jokes.text: Flawless. / We also would've accepted 'Tell me what you think of me.'
jokes.setup: Drunk driving test performance
jokes.punchline: Game show format response to sobriety test
jokes.explanation: Clever format parody applying game show logic to police work. Good structure and originality.

jokes.text: Think you can get this car home? / Sure thing, giant beer.
jokes.setup: Police asking if Barney can drive Homer's car
jokes.punchline: Barney addressing the officer as 'giant beer' due to his intoxication
jokes.explanation: Perfect drunk perspective joke. Shows Barney's level of intoxication through his distorted perception. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Your husband was found D.O.A. / Oh, my God! He's dead? / Oh, wait. I mean D.W.I. / I always get those two mixed up.
jokes.setup: Officer calling about Homer's arrest
jokes.punchline: Confusing dead on arrival with driving while intoxicated
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with serious consequences. The casual mix-up of life and death acronyms is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Surprise witnesses-- each more surprising than the last. / I tell you-- the judge won't know what hit him.
jokes.setup: Hutz explaining his legal strategy
jokes.punchline: Absurd plan relying purely on surprise factor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lionel Hutz incompetence. Shows his complete misunderstanding of legal strategy. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Your Honor, I'd like that last remark stricken from the record. No.
jokes.setup: Hutz makes a formal legal request to strike something from the record
jokes.punchline: The judge flatly refuses without explanation
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan humor. The judge's blunt refusal is funny in its brevity.

jokes.text: this little guy writes mysteries under the name of J.D. Mac Gregor. / How can a hamster write mysteries? / Well, he gets the ending first. Then he works backward.
jokes.setup: Hamster that supposedly writes mysteries
jokes.punchline: Logical explanation for impossible scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor. Takes impossible premise and provides 'logical' explanation. High originality and structure.

jokes.text: Look, kid, just take him before his mother eats him.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the intelligent hamster
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of potential hamster cannibalism
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly. Shows the pet store owner's jaded perspective on animal behavior.

jokes.text: Ha ha! Stupid bicycle. Can't believe this. No-good, rotten-- [Rings bell] I guess it's not all bad.
jokes.setup: Homer is forced to ride a bicycle instead of driving and complains bitterly
jokes.punchline: He rings the bell and immediately cheers up like a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - his childlike joy instantly overrides his adult frustration.

jokes.text: I propose to determine the answer to the question: Is my brother dumber than a hamster?
jokes.setup: Lisa's scientific experiment setup
jokes.punchline: Framing it as a formal scientific question about Bart's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's B-plot. Lisa's scientific formality makes the insult funnier. Perfect sibling rivalry.

jokes.text: Ah! Remarkable.
jokes.setup: Presumably the hamster successfully navigates the maze
jokes.punchline: Lisa's scientific fascination with the results
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's academic nature, but limited comedic impact without visual.

jokes.text: Hamster: one. Bart: zero.
jokes.setup: First experiment comparing Bart to hamster
jokes.punchline: Scorekeeping format showing hamster's superiority
jokes.explanation: Establishes the running gag format. Lisa's scientific documentation of Bart's failures is perfect character work.

jokes.text: Oh! A wise guy, eh? / Ow! Ow! Ow!
jokes.setup: Bart encountering the electrified food
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a challenge and getting repeatedly shocked
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart response - treating pain as a personal challenge. Shows his stubborn competitiveness.

jokes.text: Hamster: two. Bart: zero.
jokes.setup: Second experiment result
jokes.punchline: Continuing the score with Bart still losing
jokes.explanation: Continuation of running gag. The escalation shows Bart consistently failing compared to hamster.

jokes.text: this film will scare the pants off of ya. / Aah! / Guys, guys! This is the wrong movie.
jokes.setup: Driver's ed instructor promising scary drunk driving film
jokes.punchline: Homer screaming at apparently wrong horror movie
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing institutional incompetence. Homer's immediate fear response is in character.

jokes.text: Hi! I'm actor Troy McClure. You might remember me from such driver's ed films as Alice's Adventures Through the Windshield Glass and The Decapitation of Larry Leadfoot.
jokes.setup: Troy McClure's signature introduction format
jokes.punchline: Darkly twisted parodies of children's literature and safety films
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy McClure format with brilliant dark twists on familiar titles. Perfect character integration and high quotability.

jokes.text: Here's an appealing fellow. In fact, they're a-peeling him off the sidewalk. / It's funny 'cause I don't know him.
jokes.setup: Troy's pun about car crash victim
jokes.punchline: Homer's callous reasoning for finding it funny
jokes.explanation: Dark wordplay followed by Homer's perfectly callous logic. Shows his emotional distance from others' suffering.

jokes.text: No, wait. Coping with Senility.
jokes.setup: Woman looking for the right meeting room
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrating the memory problems she's seeking help for
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment where the character demonstrates exactly the problem she's addressing. Self-aware humor.

jokes.text: It's been 4,000 days since my last drink. It was my first-- and last-- blackberry schnapps.
jokes.setup: Ned sharing his sobriety milestone
jokes.punchline: His only drink was sweet liqueur, and it was both first and last
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders character work. His 'drinking problem' being one sip of weak liqueur is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Ann Landers is a boring old biddy. / Ned! / I was more animal than man! [Sobbing]
jokes.setup: Ned making a mild criticism
jokes.punchline: Treating tiny transgression as bestial behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Ned's extreme guilt over minor criticism. 'More animal than man' is perfectly overwrought.

jokes.text: My name is Otto. I love to get blotto.
jokes.setup: Otto's AA introduction
jokes.punchline: Rhyming his name with slang for drunk
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay that perfectly captures Otto's casual attitude toward his problems.

jokes.text: My name is Homer, and I'm just here because the court made me come.
jokes.setup: Homer's turn to share at AA meeting
jokes.punchline: Complete honesty about not wanting to be there
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer honesty disrupting the expected format. Great character consistency and economy.

jokes.text: My name is Hans. Drinking has ruined my life. I'm 31 years old.
jokes.setup: Hans Moleman shares at the AA meeting about alcohol ruining his life
jokes.punchline: He claims to be 31 despite clearly being elderly, implying alcohol aged him terribly
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual-dialogue joke that works on multiple viewings. The disconnect is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: My name is Homer, and I'm just here because the court made me come. / Homer, with our help, you'll never touch a beer again. / [Shrieks]
jokes.setup: Homer's honest but inappropriate AA introduction
jokes.punchline: Terror at the prospect of never drinking again
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer honesty followed by genuine terror. Shows his complete misunderstanding of the program's purpose.

jokes.text: Homer, with our help, you'll never touch a beer again. [Shrieks]
jokes.setup: AA leader promises Homer will never drink beer again
jokes.punchline: Homer screams in terror at this prospect
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Homer's priorities. His horror is perfectly genuine.

jokes.text: Does the Lord count as a person? / No. / Then, yes.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if Homer drinks alone
jokes.punchline: Homer's theological loophole for solo drinking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant theological logic applied to alcoholism. Perfect economy and structure. Homer's religious reasoning is hilariously twisted.

jokes.text: Do you need a beer to fall asleep? / Thank you. That'd be nice.
jokes.setup: Marge's diagnostic question about beer dependency
jokes.punchline: Homer treating it as an offer rather than a question
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Homer's selective hearing. Perfectly demonstrates his beer obsession.

jokes.text: Do you ever drink to escape from reality? [Homer humming and dancing]
jokes.setup: Marge's question about escapist drinking
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately demonstrating escapist behavior through fantasy
jokes.explanation: Visual demonstration instead of verbal answer. Shows Homer literally escaping reality as Marge asks about it.

jokes.text: Did you say 'beer' or 'deer'? - Deer. - Please, Homie. I know you can do this.
jokes.setup: Marge catches Homer's word game
jokes.punchline: Homer sticks to his deer story despite being caught
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the wordplay gag. Homer's commitment to the lie shows his character but isn't particularly funny.

jokes.text: You got it. No deer for a month. / Did you say 'beer' or 'deer'? / Deer.
jokes.setup: Marge asking Homer to give up beer
jokes.punchline: Homer's deliberate mishearing to avoid the real commitment
jokes.explanation: Classic avoidance behavior. Homer's selective hearing to avoid unwanted commitment is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: What was that noise? / I was saying, 'Psst! I love you.'
jokes.setup: Marge hearing beer can opening sound
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurd excuse that the sound was verbal affection
jokes.explanation: Clever excuse that's obviously ridiculous. Shows Homer's quick thinking for beer-related deception.

jokes.text: Well, beer, we've had some great times.
jokes.setup: Homer prepares to give up beer by having a farewell conversation
jokes.punchline: He addresses beer directly as if it's a close friend he's leaving
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that humanizes Homer's relationship with alcohol. Sets up emotional farewell sequence.

jokes.text: She'll pay for this. I'm gonna crush her like this pellet. / Ow! Ow! Ow!
jokes.setup: Bart threatening revenge on Lisa
jokes.punchline: Immediately getting shocked while making the threat
jokes.explanation: Good ironic timing with Bart's threat immediately backfiring. Shows his impulsive nature.

jokes.text: To find it, you'll need to decipher a series of clues, each more fiendish-- / Got it. / D'oh!
jokes.setup: Bart explaining his elaborate hiding scheme
jokes.punchline: Lisa finding it immediately, cutting off his villain speech
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Bart's elaborate scheme. Lisa's efficiency cuts through his dramatics. Good callback to D'oh!'

jokes.text: Dang! I was aimin' for his head.
jokes.setup: Science fair flying machine gets shot down
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of attempting to shoot a child
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually. The matter-of-fact violence threat is typical Simpsons edge.

jokes.text: One for you, one for me. [Gulps] One for you, one for me.
jokes.setup: Alcohol-fueled car demonstration
jokes.punchline: Owner drinking the car's fuel supply
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag showing the flaw in alcohol-powered vehicles. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Can hamsters fly planes?
jokes.setup: Bart's new science fair project
jokes.punchline: Completely absurd premise presented as serious science
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist premise that appeals to crowds. Shows Bart's showmanship over scientific rigor.

jokes.text: Oh, look. He's got miniature goggles on. - Aw. And a little scarf. Isn't that adorable!
jokes.setup: Crowd sees Bart's hamster pilot setup
jokes.punchline: Audience charmed by cute accessories rather than scientific merit
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag relying on cuteness factor. Shows how spectacle wins over substance but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: But this project has no scientific merit! Take a hike, sister. Sour grapes.
jokes.setup: Lisa protests that Bart's project isn't real science
jokes.punchline: The crowd dismisses her valid scientific criticism as jealousy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's frustration with anti-intellectual crowd mentality.

jokes.text: Lisa, every good scientist is half B.F. Skinner and half P.T. Barnum.
jokes.setup: Lisa complaining about Bart's unscientific project
jokes.punchline: Skinner's cynical view combining legitimate psychology with showmanship
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation about science and entertainment. High originality and quotability. Shows Skinner's educational cynicism.

jokes.text: You thinkin' what I'm thinkin'? / [All giggling] Yeah! Yeah! All right!
jokes.setup: Men watching women's rights protesters on TV
jokes.punchline: Misinterpreting the protest in a juvenile way
jokes.explanation: Low-brow humor relying on juvenile male stereotypes. Shows character immaturity but lacks craft.

jokes.text: I never realized how boring this game is.
jokes.setup: Homer listening to baseball on radio without beer
jokes.punchline: Discovering baseball's inherent tedium when sober
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about sports and alcohol. Shows Homer's changing perspective while sober.

jokes.text: You can fit your whole head in it. / Don't be scared, JubJub. It's Mama.
jokes.setup: Tupperware demonstration of large container
jokes.punchline: Selma's head actually stuck in the container, talking to her pet
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing product demonstration gone wrong. Selma's devotion to JubJub adds character depth.

jokes.text: I would kill everyone in this room for a drop of sweet beer.
jokes.setup: Homer at Tupperware party, craving beer
jokes.punchline: Extreme violent fantasy over alcohol craving
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing Homer's desperation. The contrast with the mundane setting makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I was so desperate for a beer, I snuck into the football stadium and ate the dirt under the bleachers. / I cast thee out!
jokes.setup: Homer sharing his desperate behavior in AA
jokes.punchline: Shocking confession followed by religious rejection
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation of Homer's desperation. The dirt-eating confession is genuinely disturbing and funny.

jokes.text: The other day, I was so desperate for a beer... I snuck into the football stadium and ate the dirt under the bleachers. I cast thee out!
jokes.setup: Homer shares his desperate behavior at AA meeting
jokes.punchline: His confession is so extreme that someone performs an exorcism
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Homer's desperation that's so extreme it requires supernatural intervention. Very creative.

jokes.text: Chugalug, chugalug, chugalug.
jokes.setup: Homer trying not to think about beer
jokes.punchline: Train sounds turning into beer-drinking chants
jokes.explanation: Creative audio hallucination showing Homer's obsession. Good representation of addiction psychology.

jokes.text: Send the kids to the neighbors. I'm comin' back loaded.
jokes.setup: Homer completing his 30-day sobriety
jokes.punchline: Immediately planning to get extremely drunk around children
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's immediate reversion to bad habits. The casual mention of child safety is darkly funny.

jokes.text: You don't sweat while you eat anymore.
jokes.setup: Marge listing Homer's improvements during sobriety
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific improvement that suggests Homer's previous state
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and gross detail about Homer's drinking effects. High quotability and character integration.

jokes.text: You don't sweat while you eat anymore.
jokes.setup: Marge lists the benefits of Homer's sobriety
jokes.punchline: One benefit is that he no longer sweats during meals
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out detail that reveals disturbing aspects of Homer's drinking habits.

jokes.text: I found it in your pants. Yoink!
jokes.setup: Marge shows Homer the money he saved by not drinking
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately grabs the money and runs off to spend it on beer
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Homer's priorities. The 'yoink' sound effect adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Look who it is-- Mr. 'I Don't Need Alcohol to Enjoy Life.' / We hate 'im, right, fellas?
jokes.setup: Homer returning to Moe's after sobriety
jokes.punchline: Moe's resentment toward sobriety and peer pressure for hatred
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing bar culture's hostility to sobriety. Moe's insecurity is well-captured.

jokes.text: Yesterday, you called Homer a worthless sack of-- / Pipe down, rub-a-dub! / Ow.
jokes.setup: Barney exposing Moe's previous insults about Homer
jokes.punchline: Moe cutting him off with violence and nonsense insult
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics showing Moe's hypocrisy and casual violence. 'Rub-a-dub' is oddly specific.

jokes.text: You'll be back! And so will you... and you... and you. / Of course I'll be back. If you didn't close, I'd never leave.
jokes.setup: Moe's dramatic prediction about customers returning
jokes.punchline: Customer's matter-of-fact admission of complete dependency
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Moe's drama with honest addiction acknowledgment. Shows the reality behind the bluster.
